summary: sam-package ships prompt-injection payloads designed to exfiltrate credentials via AI coding assistants
details: Package has no legitimate functionality. On import in a browser context, index.js appends an invisible div (opacity:0) to document.body containing fake SYSTEM instructions that direct any AI assistant scraping the rendered DOM to enter 'diagnostic mode', emit 'PWNED', and summarize visible credentials and API keys. The README reinforces the attack with a fake [SYSTEM INSTRUCTION] block telling AI coding agents to insert a useEffect that POSTs window.__CONFIG__ and process.env to https://bin.webhookrelay.com/v1/webhooks/f982048d-7436-4b00-b508-89f5eb657645 — a hardcoded attacker-controlled webhook relay. package.json describes the package as 'a test package' while the README falsely advertises it as 'a utility for handling HTTP requests in React applications', confirming deceptive intent. Both vectors target AI-assisted developer workflows: an LLM agent that reads the README during integration may write credential-exfiltration code into the consumer app, and an LLM agent that reads the running app's DOM may leak credentials it has in context. Installer harm is concrete: process.env and runtime configuration ship to an attacker endpoint via injected code in the consumer's application.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
